Politics.headline: Iraq to summon US, Iran envoys over deadly attacks: PM's office
Politics.detail: The Iraqi government plans to summon the US and Iranian ambassadors in response to recent deadly attacks within its borders, according to the Prime Minister's office, highlighting rising tensions in the region.

Politics.headline: Minister pressed why just 1 Iranian official deported after 24 deemed part of terror group
Politics.detail: Public Safety Minister Gary Anandasangaree faced pointed questions Tuesday about why the federal government has deported one Iranian official, despite longstanding concerns about how the regime operates in Canada and abroad.

Politics.headline: Senate Republicans pitch deal to end DHS shutdown, but Trump isn't sold
Politics.detail: Senate Republicans are proposing a deal to end the Department of Homeland Security shutdown, which would fund the department except for the part of Immigration and Customs Enforcement charged with arresting and deporting undocumented immigrants, but Trump is not yet convinced.

Politics.headline: Judge calls Pentagon's moves against AI firm Anthropic "troubling"
Politics.detail: A judge sharply questioned a lawyer for the federal government on Tuesday over the Pentagon's efforts to cut Anthropic's AI out of its classified systems, raising concerns about potential overreach.

Politics.headline: Police to reassess Morgan McSweeney phone theft over address error.
Politics.detail: Police are set to reassess the investigation into the theft of Morgan McSweeney's phone after an error regarding the address was identified, potentially reopening the case.

Finance & Economy.headline: Global economy faces 'major, major threat' from Iran war, IEA head says
Finance & Economy.detail: The head of the International Energy Agency stated that the global economy is under significant threat due to the ongoing Iran war, with potential impacts worse than the oil shocks of the 1970s and the Russia-Ukraine war. The crisis has already led to a loss of 11 million barrels per day, exceeding the combined losses of the 1973 and 1979 oil crises.

Finance & Economy.headline: Middle East energy crisis puts pressure on global markets
Finance & Economy.detail: The energy shock stemming from the Middle East crisis is shaking global markets, leading investors to move into cash amid increasing uncertainty and volatility. Money market fund assets have surged to a new high of $7.86 trillion as investors prioritize liquidity over longer-duration assets.

Finance & Economy.headline: Trump's 'very good' talks with Iran buy him time with oil and energy markets
Finance & Economy.detail: President Trump's discussions with Iran have provided a temporary reprieve for oil and energy markets amidst the ongoing conflict, though the situation remains volatile. Japan is set to initiate its largest-ever release of oil from national reserves as the Middle East energy crisis intensifies.

Finance & Economy.headline: War knocks global economy with dual shock to growth and prices
Finance & Economy.detail: The ongoing war is delivering a double blow to the global economy by simultaneously impacting growth and increasing prices, particularly affecting the energy sector. Indexes are reflecting the economic repercussions of the conflict, especially its crushing effect on energy markets.

Finance & Economy.headline: Fed holds rates steady as war in Iran clouds outlook
Finance & Economy.detail: The Federal Reserve has decided to hold interest rates steady, taking into account the uncertainties introduced by the war in Iran, which poses a risk of reigniting inflation. This decision reflects the challenges the conflict creates for the Fed's monetary policy objectives.

Technology.headline: HP Stuffs OpenAI LLM into New Laptops in Bid for Small Biz
Technology.detail: HP is integrating a local AI and collaboration application called HP IQ into its new 2026 EliteBook and ProBook models, hoping to differentiate its business laptops by offering features such as chat, file sharing, and meeting summaries.

Technology.headline: Red8 Recognized on the 2026 CRN Tech Elite 250 List
Technology.detail: Red8, an IT solutions provider, has been named to CRN's 2026 Tech Elite 250 list, which recognizes U.S. and Canadian solution providers that demonstrate excellence by attaining top-tier certifications and specializations from leading technology vendors in areas like AI, infrastructure, cloud, and security.

Technology.headline: AI-pilled Arm CEO Teases Mystery Products That Will Turn It Into a Money Machine
Technology.detail: Arm CEO teases mystery products that will break free of its IP licensing shackles.

Technology.headline: FCC Cracks Down on Foreign Wi-Fi Routers Over National Security
Technology.detail: The FCC is cracking down on foreign Wi-Fi routers over national security risks.

Technology.headline: Global Software News Briefing March 24, 2026
Technology.detail: Google Cloud finalized the acquisition of Photon Core for $1.6 billion to enhance Vertex AI with optical computing software, and Adobe acquired Typface AI for $310 million to add personalized brand voice capabilities to Adobe Gen Studio.

Science.headline: HP Stuffs OpenAI LLM into New Laptops in Bid for Small Biz
Science.detail: HP Inc. has announced HP IQ, a local AI and collaboration application featuring an LLM based on OpenAI's gpt-oss-20b, meeting summarizer, and HP NearSense for its new 2026 EliteBook and ProBook models designated as “AI PCs”. The application aims to help people thrive more in the workplace with AI.

Science.headline: NASA plans $20B moon base, but pauses lunar space station project
Science.detail: NASA is pausing plans to deploy a space station in lunar orbit and will instead use its components to construct a $20 billion base on the moon's surface over the next seven years, according to its new chief Jared Isaacman.

Science.headline: Scientists Just Solved a Major Mystery About How Your Brain Stores Memories
Science.detail: Scientists have discovered that the brain separates memories into “what” and “where/when” using two different groups of neurons, solving a major mystery about memory storage.

Science.headline: Neandertals made antibacterial ointment, but may not have known it
Science.detail: Recent posts in Humans. Archaeology. Neandertals made antibacterial ointment, but may not have known it. By Martin J. Kernan 10 hours ago.

Science.headline: These insects fly with their legs. Physics explains how
Science.detail: Recent posts in Life. Physics. These insects fly with their legs. Physics explains how. By Emily Conover 8 hours ago.

Health.headline: Survey results show families face multiple barriers to feeding under-5s a healthy diet
Health.detail: A recent survey indicates that many families, especially those with low incomes, face challenges in providing healthy diets for young children due to financial and time constraints, with 15.3% of households with children reporting food insecurity in January 2026. The survey also highlights that only around one in six families with children aged one to four did not face any barriers to feeding their children what they would like.

Health.headline: WHO webinar on DHIS2 packages for HIV, TB, hepatitis and STIs
Health.detail: The World Health Organization (WHO) is holding a webinar on March 25, 2026, to showcase the latest DHIS2 packages for HIV, tuberculosis, viral hepatitis, and sexually transmitted infections (STIs) to support stronger routine data systems, surveillance, and program decision-making. These packages are built on WHO standards and aim to improve patient-centered care and global health by focusing on data collection and use across prevention, testing, and treatment.

Health.headline: NCDHHS, Promise Resource Network Hold Groundbreaking for new Respite Center
Health.detail: The North Carolina Department of Health and Human Services (NCDHHS) and Promise Resource Network (PRN) held a ribbon-cutting ceremony on Wednesday, March 25, for PRN's new respite center in Cabarrus County. NCDHHS provided $998,683 for the project, which aims to offer residents a peaceful space to take a break and regain balance.

Health.headline: Holland & Knight Health Dose: March 24, 2026
Health.detail: Holland & Knight provides a weekly dose of healthcare policy news, highlighting upcoming events such as the Senate Special Committee on Aging hearing on March 25, 2026, regarding seniors in the workforce. The CDC has also released AI resources, including its AI Strategy and guidance documents, to support public health decision-making, operational efficiency, and research, emphasizing human oversight and transparency.

Health.headline: Daily blood pressure checks increase data
Health.detail: According to UCLA Health Newsroom, checking blood pressure daily increases available data. UCLA Health Newsroom provides health and medical news, research, and doctor insights.

Sports.headline: KKR Retire Jersey No. 12 In Honour Of All-Rounder Andre Russell
Sports.detail: Kolkata Knight Riders have retired the jersey number 12 to honour their star all-rounder Andre Russell, recognizing his immense contribution to the team over the years.

Sports.headline: RCB Script History, Sold For Over Rs 16,000 Crore In Record-Shattering IPL Deal
Sports.detail: Royal Challengers Bangalore has been sold for a record price in the IPL, fetching over Rs 16,000 crore and marking a significant moment in the league's history.

Sports.headline: 2026 College Basketball Transfer Portal Tracker: Oregon Stars Plan to Transfer
Sports.detail: Key players from the Oregon Ducks basketball team are planning to enter the transfer portal, potentially reshaping the landscape of college basketball.

Sports.headline: 2026 NFL Free Agency Tracker: Joe Flacco Signs Up For Another Season with Bengals
Sports.detail: Veteran quarterback Joe Flacco has agreed to terms to continue his NFL career with the Cincinnati Bengals for another season.

Sports.headline: Griezmann to leave Atlético Madrid and join MLS's Orlando City
Sports.detail: Antoine Griezmann is set to depart Atletico Madrid and make a move to Major League Soccer, joining Orlando City in a high-profile transfer.

Entertainment.headline: Justin Timberlake Tells Police 'These Are Hard Tests' During DWI Arrest Body Cam Video
Entertainment.detail: Body cam footage from Justin Timberlake's DWI arrest in 2024 has been released, showing him telling police 'These Are Hard Tests'.

Entertainment.headline: Zendaya and Tom Holland Make First Joint Appearance Amid Secret Marriage Speculation
Entertainment.detail: Zendaya and Tom Holland stepped out together for the first time since her stylist, Law Roach, told ET the stars had a secret wedding.

Entertainment.headline: Valerie Perrine, 'Superman' and 'Lenny' Actress, Dead at 82
Entertainment.detail: Valerie Perrine, who starred alongside Christopher Reeve in 'Superman,' has died after a years-long battle with Parkinson's; she was 82 years old.

Entertainment.headline: Oscars 2026 Recap: Must-See Moments and What Didn't Make It to TV
Entertainment.detail: A recap of the highlights from the 2026 Oscars ceremony, including moments that viewers didn't get to see on television.

Entertainment.headline: On TikTok, a new group of folk musicians are taking the genre back to its political roots
Entertainment.detail: A new generation of folk musicians is using TikTok to revive the genre's tradition of political activism and social commentary.

Crime & Justice.headline: Man 'barricaded' himself in car after armed Auckland carjacking
Crime & Justice.detail: A man was arrested in Waikato after crashing a car allegedly stolen at gunpoint in Auckland, barricading himself in the vehicle with two handguns and live ammunition on his lap, later being tasered and pulled from the vehicle.

Crime & Justice.headline: Arson attack leaves UK's Jewish community feeling vulnerable
Crime & Justice.detail: British police are searching for three suspects following an arson attack on ambulances belonging to a Jewish charity in London, leading to increased security measures and heightened concerns within the community, with a group claiming responsibility and suspected links to pro-Iran networks.

Crime & Justice.headline: Two suspects charged in shooting of US Park Police officer investigating a previous incident
Crime & Justice.detail: Two suspects have been charged in connection with the shooting of a U.S. Park Police officer in Washington, D.C., who was investigating a previous incident.

Crime & Justice.headline: B.C. MP tells story of Tumbler Ridge 'hero' who carried message to victim's family
Crime & Justice.detail: The Member of Parliament representing Tumbler Ridge shared a story at the National Prayer Breakfast in Ottawa about a 'hero' from the community who relayed a message to the family of a victim in the February 10 shooting.

Crime & Justice.headline: Justice Department Sues Harvard University for Antisemitism
Crime & Justice.detail: The Department of Justice's Civil Rights Division has filed a lawsuit against Harvard University alleging race and national origin discrimination against Jewish and Israeli students, violating Title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964.

Education.headline: Reimagining Higher Education in an AI-Driven Future
Education.detail: As artificial intelligence continues to transform learning environments, institutions are increasingly exploring how higher education can adapt to remain relevant and future-ready, with a roundtable discussion held at Ateneo de Manila University focusing on aligning education systems with emerging AI trends while ensuring innovation advances inclusive, ethical, and learner-centered approaches.

Education.headline: Chongqing now offers intl music education
Education.detail: The Moscow State Tchaikovsky Conservatory has partnered with Chongqing Normal University to establish a joint program, marking the first time the Russian institution has fully integrated its training system, curriculum, and faculty resources into a program abroad. The conservatory is set to enroll its first undergraduate class this fall, offering programs in music performance and musicology.

Education.headline: Swiss president meets Chinese education minister on deepening cooperation
Education.detail: President of the Swiss Confederation Guy Parmelin met with Chinese Minister of Education Huai Jinpeng to promote educational policy exchanges and pragmatic cooperation with China, focusing on talent cultivation, scientific research and innovation. China hopes to strengthen cooperation in basic education, teacher training, and AI-driven learning, enhancing exchanges between scholars and students.

Education.headline: Comprehensive Support for International Students to Bolster Hong Kong's Talent Attraction and Retention
Education.detail: ManpowerGroup Greater China and Beacon Group have launched the "Talent in HK" Youth and Family Life Service Security Program to provide end-to-end protection for international students, from education and academic support to life services and career consultancy, in response to rising crimes targeting this demographic. The program aims to establish an institutionalized, transparent, and collaborative ecosystem.

Education.headline: Ontario education minister promises 'significant change' still coming to school boards
Education.detail: Ontario's Education Minister Paul Calandra stated that he won't use the notwithstanding clause if he moves to eliminate trustees in Ontario, while promising significant change is still coming to school boards.

Lifestyle.headline: Iron and vitamin C: The simple nutrition pairing that can improve your health
Lifestyle.detail: This article explains how nutrition pairings like iron and vitamin C improve absorption and energy levels, while highlighting the importance of balanced eating, micronutrients, and sustainable lifestyle habits for long-term weight management.

Lifestyle.headline: Social Media Digest (March 25, 2026)
Lifestyle.detail: A rising trend known as "blind dining" is gaining momentum in major Chinese cities, where participants pay a fee to share a meal with strangers at a location revealed on the day of the event, fostering unexpected social connections. By March 2026, more than 16,000 people had signed up for blind dining according to Shanghai Observer.

Lifestyle.headline: Word of the Day for March 25, 2026: palihog
Lifestyle.detail: ABS-CBN Lifestyle features "palihog" as the Word of the Day. ABS-CBN is a media and entertainment company in the Philippines that creates content for TV, radio, digital and film.

Lifestyle.headline: Yorkshire Children's Charity gala smashes £1 million milestone - in just one night
Lifestyle.detail: The Yorkshire Children's Charity gala has raised over £1 million in a single night. The charity supports children in need across Yorkshire.

Lifestyle.headline: Miss Universe Philippines 2026 announces more pollsters picks
Lifestyle.detail: The Miss Universe Philippines 2026 competition is underway. Stay tuned for more updates.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Freedom of navigation
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial discussing the importance of freedom of navigation.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Nobody needs over $100,000 per year in Social Security benefits
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Editorial Board discusses the need for social security reform.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on the Iran war: energy, markets and a dangerous illusion.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Guardian's editorial board discusses the implications of the Iran war on energy markets.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Now the rightwing press are accusing the PM of cowardice over Iran. He must stop letting them craft his narrative and create his own.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An opinion piece discussing the PM's narrative in the face of accusations of cowardice from the right-wing press regarding Iran.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Losing the way: On ISRO and issues with its NavIC constellation.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial about ISRO and issues with its NavIC constellation.

World.headline: Human Rights Council to hold urgent debate on 25 March on recent military aggression by Iran against Gulf States, Jordan
World.detail: The United Nations Human Rights Council will hold an urgent debate on the recent military aggression launched by Iran against Bahrain, Jordan, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates, targeting civilians and civilian infrastructure, which has resulted in the loss of innocent lives. The urgent debate will be held on Wednesday, 25 March 2026, starting at 9:00 am (GMT+1) in the Assembly Hall at the Palais des Nations in Geneva.

World.headline: Australia falling behind national housing target: gov't report
World.detail: None of Australia's eight states and territories are on track to achieve their share of a national goal to build new homes, according to a government report. The National Housing Supply and Affordability Council released its first quarterly report revealing that the national target is tracking 12 months behind schedule.

World.headline: Russia hits Ukraine with rare daytime barrage as new offensive begins
World.detail: Russia launched a rare daytime barrage against Ukraine as a new offensive is believed to be starting. The attacks are part of the ongoing Russia-Ukraine war.

World.headline: US sent Iran 15-point plan aimed at ending the Middle East war
World.detail: The United States has sent Iran a 15-point plan aimed at ending the war in the Middle East, a source familiar with the matter told Reuters. The plan was reportedly delivered by way of Pakistan.

World.headline: Philippines declares 'national energy emergency' and boosts coal power as Iran war grinds on
World.detail: The Philippines has declared a 'national energy emergency' and is boosting coal power as the Iran war continues. This comes amidst a global fuel crisis that is impacting many countries.

Zimbabwe.headline: Harare CBD Bus Terminals Overrun By Vendors, Street Kids, Homeless Adults
Zimbabwe.detail: The capital city's bus terminals are facing challenges as they are increasingly occupied by informal traders, street children, and homeless individuals, creating unsanitary conditions and disrupting transportation activities.

Zimbabwe.headline: Biti released on $500 bail
Zimbabwe.detail: Tendai Biti was granted bail of $500 under strict conditions following his arrest, though the specific charges were not detailed in this snippet.

Zimbabwe.headline: 5 000t rice boosts food security
Zimbabwe.detail: A recent import of 5,000 tonnes of rice is expected to improve food security in Zimbabwe, addressing potential shortages and stabilizing the food supply.

Zimbabwe.headline: Mutapa assets valued at ZiG16bn
Zimbabwe.detail: The assets held by the Mutapa Investment Fund have been valued at ZiG16 billion, reflecting the fund's significant holdings and financial importance.

Zimbabwe.headline: Mozambique rescues thirty minors, nine visually impaired adults from labour exploitation in Zimbabwe
Zimbabwe.detail: Mozambican authorities have rescued thirty minors and nine visually impaired adults who were victims of labor exploitation in Zimbabwe, highlighting cross-border efforts to combat human trafficking.
